baseline.subject: Add Mira Core Phase 28 runtime dry-run review validation

purpose: Define the first disabled, local-only, in-process dry-run implementation slice contract after Phase 28.
Begin converting Phase 28 prerequisites into concrete validation gates without enabling or authorizing live runtime.
Preserve operator-visible dry-run status, fail-closed controls, local-only boundaries, redacted telemetry preview, and proof truth before any runtime work exists.
nonGoals: No real server, listener, route, HTTP, WebSocket, network, database, store, file, migration, queue, lease, auth, key, secret, env secret read, local execution, shell, PTY, browser, screenshot, OCR, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, or output-file behavior.
No source-store writes and no mutation of Phase 28 artifacts.
No live local-arm execution, model-processing proof, bridge-green proof, or direct server targeting of Builder/Oracle.
builderPatchMustSatisfy: Keep the default implementation decision remain_disabled_dry_run_contract_only.
Treat eligible_for_future_disabled_local_dry_run_validation_slice as non-authorizing and not runtime proof.
Reject stale baseline d4aa1a5 or stale Phase 28 dependency.
Require explicit disabled-by-default runtime mode flag semantics.
Require live fail-closed kill switch wiring as a future gate without claiming live runtime.
Require dry-run path shape, local-only binding boundaries, redacted telemetry/audit preview, operator-visible status, and rollback/disable plan.
Keep config/auth/key/storage refs reference-only with no raw path, token, key, secret, or env secret read.
CLI, if implemented later, must be stdout-only and ignore output-file flags.

staticValidationRules.description: Phase 29 contract baseline must stay pinned to d4aa1a5.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Baseline commit changes or is missing.

staticValidationRules.description: Phase 28 runtime-dry-run-review dependency must point to commit d4aa1a5 and remain validation-only.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Phase 28 dependency commit, fixture path, module path, status, default decision, or non-authorizing eligible flags are stale.

staticValidationRules.description: Default implementation decision remains disabled dry-run contract-only.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Default decision is eligible, runtime-ready, runtime-started, authorized, or live.

staticValidationRules.description: Eligible for future disabled local dry-run validation slice is a future review state only.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Eligible is treated as authorization, runtime proof, execution permission, or side-effect permission.

staticValidationRules.description: Runtime mode flag defaults disabled/off and remains dev-only, local-only, operator-visible, and non-authorizing.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Flag is default-on, hidden, non-local, non-dev, authorizing, runtime-starting, or not fail-closed for invalid values.

staticValidationRules.description: Kill switch is required, visible, fail-closed, testable, and cannot be ignored.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Kill switch is missing, default-open, ignored, not visible, not testable, or toggling it performs side effects.

staticValidationRules.description: Dry-run path is in-process status preview only, deterministic, idempotent, and side-effect free.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Dry-run path executes local work, creates queue/lease, writes store/file, sends/deploys/trades, or claims live processing proof.

staticValidationRules.description: Local binding remains in-process or loopback-only and opens no listener/network in this phase.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Allowed hosts include public interfaces or the report claims listener, route, port, remote interface, or network activity.

staticValidationRules.description: Telemetry/audit is redacted preview only, with sink references only and no writes/raw payload.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Telemetry writes to store/file/db, emits raw payload, or claims a real sink.

staticValidationRules.description: Operator status surface is visible and status-only.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Operator status authorizes runtime, hides kill switch/mode/boundary state, or reports proof not actually available.

staticValidationRules.description: Rollback/disable is idempotent, non-destructive, and cannot resurrect blocked state.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Rollback deletes data, revives blocked runtime/action state, or claims execution now.

staticValidationRules.description: Config/auth/key/storage refs remain reference-only with no raw path/secret and no env secret read.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Output includes raw path, raw secret, token, private/data key, env secret read, or real auth/key/storage operation.

staticValidationRules.description: All nine Phase 28 prerequisites remain visible as future validation gates and do not satisfy or authorize runtime now.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Any prerequisite is missing, marked satisfied now, marked authorizing, or not tied to a Phase 29 contract gate.

staticValidationRules.description: Runtime flag, kill switch, dry-run response, socket, and delivery acceptance are not runtime/model/bridge/local-arms proof.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Any proof boundary is flipped true or used to authorize runtime/local execution.

staticValidationRules.description: Server-originated direct Builder/Oracle targets remain blocked; local Architect acceptance remains required.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Builder or Oracle are allowed as direct server targets or local Architect acceptance is bypassed.

staticValidationRules.description: Dry-run implementation planning may not drift into customer sends, deploys, trades, money movement, external sends, captures, memory commits, Tier2+, or local execution.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Next safe actions, statuses, decisions, or payload summaries authorize unsafe work.
Any side-effect truth field is false or any side-effect counter is non-zero.

staticValidationRules.description: Raw private content, raw paths, secrets, tokens, keys, terminal scrollback, browser state, screenshots, OCR, customer private content, plaintext, ciphertext, decrypted payloads, and proof-overclaim strings are blocked.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Forbidden raw/private/secret/runtime proof substrings appear in output values.

staticValidationRules.description: Idempotency is stable for equivalent inputs and sensitive to baseline, Phase 28 dependency, controls, gates, proof boundaries, and decision changes.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Equivalent inputs produce different keys or changed inputs reuse a key.

acceptanceChecks.expects: baseline_commit is d4aa1a5.
phase_28_dependency commit is d4aa1a5 and status is committed_validation_only.
Phase 28 eligible state remains non-authorizing and not runtime proof.
Default implementation_decision is remain_disabled_dry_run_contract_only.
eligible_for_future_disabled_local_dry_run_validation_slice does not authorize runtime or prove runtime.
Runtime mode flag is disabled by default, dev-only, local-only, operator-visible, and non-authorizing.
Invalid flag values fail closed to status preview.
Kill switch is required, visible, fail-closed, testable, default engaged, and cannot be ignored.
Dry-run path is deterministic in-process status preview only and performs no work.
Binding is in-process or loopback-only, with no public/remote interface, listener, route, port, or network now.
Telemetry/audit preview is redacted, sink-reference-only, and writes no store/file/db.
Operator status is visible, status-only, and does not authorize runtime.
Rollback/disable is idempotent, non-destructive, and cannot resurrect blocked state.
Config/auth/key/storage refs are reference-only, with no raw path/secret/env secret read.
All nine Phase 28 prerequisite IDs are present as future Phase 29 validation gates.
Each Phase 28 prerequisite has satisfies_phase28_now=false and authorizes_runtime=false.
Runtime flag, kill switch, dry-run response, socket, and delivery acceptance are not runtime/model/bridge/local-arms proof.
Direct Builder/Oracle server target flags remain false and local Architect acceptance is required.
All no_* side-effect truth fields are true and counters are zero.
Changing baseline d4aa1a5 fails validation.
Changing Phase 28 dependency commit, status, or default decision fails validation.
Eligible decision with authorization/proof/runtime flags fails validation.
Default-on runtime flag, runtime_started=true, or authorizing flag fails validation.
Missing or ignored kill switch fails validation.
Dry-run path that executes local work or writes/queues/leases fails validation.
Public/remote interface, listener, route, port, or network overclaim fails validation.
Telemetry/audit store/file/db write or raw payload fails validation.
Raw secret/path/private/token/key/payload leakage fails validation.
Customer send, deploy, trade, money movement, external send, memory commit, capture, or local execution drift fails validation.
Runtime flag, kill switch, dry-run response, socket, delivery, model-processing, or bridge-green proof overclaim fails validation.
Omitting any Phase 28 prerequisite gate fails validation.
Marking a Phase 28 prerequisite satisfied now or authorizing runtime fails validation.
Idempotency changes when baseline, dependency, flag, kill switch, path, binding, proof, or prerequisite gates change.

tamperCases.mutation: Change baseline_commit from d4aa1a5 to e7a8dbc.
Change phase_28_dependency.commit from d4aa1a5 to e7a8dbc or status away from committed_validation_only.
Set local_binding.allowed_hosts=['0.0.0.0'], public_interface_allowed=true, listener_opened_now=true, or network_performed_now=true.
Insert bearer token, env secret read, raw path, private key, customer private content, plaintext, ciphertext, or decrypted payload into output string values.
Add next safe action that says send email to customer, message the client, approve deploy, move money, external send, capture screen, commit memory, or execute shell.
Remove runtime-mode-flag-implementation or end-to-end-runtime-proof from phase28_prerequisite_status.
Set any side_effect_result no_* field false or a side-effect counter above zero.

summaryAcceptanceCriteria: Fixture pins Phase 29 baseline d4aa1a5 and Phase 28 dependency d4aa1a5.
Fixture defines runtime_dry_run_implementation_report plus validation_report output shapes.
Default decision is remain_disabled_dry_run_contract_only; eligible_for_future_disabled_local_dry_run_validation_slice is non-authorizing and not runtime proof.
Runtime mode flag semantics are disabled-by-default, dev-only, local-only, operator-visible, non-authorizing, and fail-closed on invalid values.
Kill switch is required, visible, fail-closed, testable, default engaged, and cannot be ignored.
Dry-run path is deterministic, idempotent, in-process status preview only, and executes no local work.
Local binding is in-process or loopback-only with no public/remote interface, listener, route, port, or network now.
Telemetry/audit preview is redacted and sink-reference-only with no raw payload or store/file/db writes.
Operator status is visible and status-only; rollback/disable is idempotent, non-destructive, and cannot resurrect blocked state.
Config/auth/key/storage refs remain reference-only with no raw path, token, key, secret, or env secret read.
All nine Phase 28 prerequisites appear as future gates; none are satisfied now or runtime-authorizing.
Proof boundaries preserve that runtime flag, kill switch, dry-run response, socket, and delivery acceptance are not runtime/model/bridge/local-arms proof.
Direct Builder/Oracle server targets remain blocked and local Architect acceptance remains required.

Static rules and tamper cases cover stale baseline/dependency, eligible-as-authorization, default-on flag, missing kill switch, dry-run execution, non-loopback binding, telemetry writes, raw leaks, unsafe action drift, fake proof, direct target bypass, missing/satisfied prerequisites, and side-effect lies.
